# UC-17.1.16 · MAC Authentication Bypass Anomaly Detection

## Description

Complements UC-17.1.6 whitelist checks with **volume and velocity** anomalies (sudden MAB spikes per port or site) that may indicate MAC spoofing or policy gaps.

## Value

Complements UC-17.1.6 whitelist checks with **volume and velocity** anomalies (sudden MAB spikes per port or site) that may indicate MAC spoofing or policy gaps.

## Implementation

Baseline MAB events per switch/port; alert on spikes. Join UC-17.1.6 for unknown MAC focus.

## Detailed Implementation

Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: `Splunk_TA_cisco-ise`, `TA-cisco_ios`, HPE Aruba CX syslog.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `cisco:ise:auth` with MAB.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
Baseline MAB events per switch/port; alert on spikes. Join UC-17.1.6 for unknown MAC focus.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=nac sourcetype="cisco:ise:auth" auth_method="MAB" earliest=-7d
| bin _time span=1h
| stats count by _time, switch, port
| eventstats avg(count) as baseline by switch
| where count > baseline*3 AND count>10
| sort -count
```

Understanding this SPL

**MAC Authentication Bypass Anomaly Detection** — Complements UC-17.1.6 whitelist checks with **volume and velocity** anomalies (sudden MAB spikes per port or site) that may indicate MAC spoofing or policy gaps.

Documented **Data sources**: `cisco:ise:auth` with MAB. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): `Splunk_TA_cisco-ise`, `TA-cisco_ios`, HPE Aruba CX syslog. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: nac; **sourcetype**: cisco:ise:auth. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=nac, sourcetype="cisco:ise:auth", time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• Discretizes time or numeric ranges with `bin`/`bucket`.
• `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by _time, switch, port** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions.
• `eventstats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by switch** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions.
• Filters the current rows with `where count > baseline*3 AND count>10` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• Orders rows with `sort` — combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.

Step 3 — Validate
Compare with Cisco ISE Live Log and endpoint details for the same sessions, MACs, and time range.


Step 4 — Operationalize
Step 4 — Operationalize
Step 4 — Operationalize
Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Line chart (MAB rate per switch), Table (spike events), Heatmap (port × hour).

## SPL

```spl
index=nac sourcetype="cisco:ise:auth" auth_method="MAB" earliest=-7d
| bin _time span=1h
| stats count by _time, switch, port
| eventstats avg(count) as baseline by switch
| where count > baseline*3 AND count>10
| sort -count
```

## CIM SPL

```spl
| tstats `summariesonly` count
  from datamodel=Authentication.Authentication
  by Authentication.user, Authentication.action, Authentication.src span=1h
| sort - count
```

## Visualization

Line chart (MAB rate per switch), Table (spike events), Heatmap (port × hour).
